title: Wrapper advertises Base UI className callback support but drops callback classes
reasoning: The component is typed as the full Base UI SeparatorPrimitive.Props surface, where className can be a state callback. Passing className through cn treats it as a static class value, so a valid caller-provided className function will not be invoked by the primitive and its returned classes will be lost. This makes the wrapper's TypeScript contract broader than its runtime behavior.
reproduction: Call <Separator className={({ orientation }) => orientation === "vertical" ? "my-vertical" : "my-horizontal"} />. The wrapper consumes that function in cn instead of forwarding a function to SeparatorPrimitive, so the state-dependent class is not applied.
recommendation: Either narrow the wrapper prop type so className only accepts static class values, or preserve Base UI's callback form by passing a className function to SeparatorPrimitive that combines the base classes with the caller callback result.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are listed for this component, and there is no test exercising the callback form inherited from SeparatorPrimitive.Props.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test that passes a className callback and verifies the rendered separator includes the callback's returned class for the current orientation.
minimumFixScope: Adjust frontend/src/components/ui/separator.tsx prop typing or className forwarding logic, plus a focused callback className regression test.
